summary: Good‑quality matrix recording captures a solid first set and uneven second set, highlighted by standout performances of Minglewood Blues, Sugaree, Morning Dew and strong Jerry Garcia work.
review: The Sir‑Mick matrix blend softens the usual harshness of a soundboard and adds natural reverb, delivering a clear and listenable mix that most reviewers praised. Musically the show shines in its opening set – Minglewood Blues, Sugaree, Friend of the Devil and an early Brent‑era keyboard sweep are repeatedly lauded – while the second set is more mixed, with Althea, Terrapin and Ship dragging down the flow but a soaring Morning Dew rescuing the night. Overall sentiment is mixed to positive.

ai_rating.rationale: Five detailed reviews give an average of 4.4 stars; most praise the matrix recording and many songs, while a few note performance flaws. Weighted toward show quality (70%) the composite rating lands in the strong‑good range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Softened SBD board with natural reverb blends well with audience; minor pitch adjustments needed (-1% to +2%) and occasional muddy mix on certain tracks, but overall clearer than pure SBD sources.

standout_songs: China Cat > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is consistently strong and tight, showcasing Brent-era keyboard flourishes and Jerry's improvisational flair. The second set is uneven – a sluggish Althea start and weaker moments on Terrapin and Ship are offset by an inspiring jam into Space and a superb Morning Dew that lifts the close.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for snarling tones, adventurous snaking figures, beautiful vocal tone on Morning Dew, and strong interplay with the rhythm section.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for slide work on Minglewood Blues and rhythmic attacks that complement Jerry’s leads.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent's sweeping flourishes are highlighted as a key part of the early‑'80s sound, especially in the first set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill is commended for picking up lost beats, resetting the band confidently, though some reviewers felt the drumming on later songs was less engaging.
